page: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server designed for high-performance computing workloads. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The server achieved top-tier performance in large-block read-only memory bandwidth at 339.55 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 564.52 GB/sec. It also demonstrated top-tier results in software compilation using Clang and Passmark CPU extended instructions. Redis SET operations reached a top-tier throughput of 117.53 million operations per second. Single-core performance across stress-ng, Geekbench, and compression tasks remains in the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hpc6id.32xlarge offers high resource density by combining dedicated compute cores, massive local NVMe storage, and high-speed networking on the Nitro hypervisor. The primary tradeoff is the moderate single-core performance relative to its exceptional multi-threaded and memory bandwidth capabilities. This hardware profile is highly suited for high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale database operations, software compilation pipelines, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is an x86_64 high-performance computing server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the server delivers top-tier multi-threaded performance, notably in large-block memory read bandwidth at 339.55 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 564.52 GB/sec. It also excels in database operations, achieving a top-tier Redis SET throughput of 117.53 million operations per second. While single-core benchmarks are average, its massive parallel processing power and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for high-performance computing, software compilation, and large-scale database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge is a high-performance server with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, 15.2 TB NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for intensive parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc6id.32xlarge features 64 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 1024 GB RAM, and 15.2 TB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance computing.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core Intel Xeon server with 15.2 TB NVMe storage and 200 Gbps networking for high-performance parallel computing.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores running at 3.5 GHz
1024 GB of DDR4 memory and 15,200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-threaded decompression and large-block memory read speeds
Optimized for high-performance computing, databases, and software compilation
